source_title: AI, Machine Learning, Deep Learning and Generative AI Explained

concepts.name: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
concepts.definition: The simulation of human intelligence by machines to match or exceed human capabilities, including learning, reasoning, and inference.

concepts.name: Machine Learning
concepts.definition: A subset of AI where machines are trained using data to recognize patterns and make predictions without explicit programming.

concepts.name: Deep Learning
concepts.definition: A subset of machine learning that uses neural networks with multiple layers to simulate the human brain's functioning for pattern recognition and decision-making.

concepts.name: Generative AI
concepts.definition: A subset of AI focused on generating new content, such as text, images, or audio, using foundation models like large language models.

concepts.name: Foundation Models
concepts.definition: Large-scale models trained on vast datasets that serve as the basis for generative AI applications, such as large language models.

concepts.name: Large Language Models
concepts.definition: Foundation models trained on language data to predict and generate text, sentences, or even entire documents.

gotchas.description: Generative AI is not truly generative but rather recombines existing information.
gotchas.context: When generating new content using generative AI models.
gotchas.solution: Accept that generative AI creates new permutations of existing data, similar to composing music from existing notes.
